scope_note: QA Counterfactual Descent Cert family [265]: third sharp-claim cert derived from the Kochenderfer 2026 'Algorithms for Validation' bridge (Kochenderfer, 2026; docs/specs/QA_KOCHENDERFER_BRIDGE.md §5 counterfactual-explanation row). CLAIM (narrow): for QA finite orbit-class PASS/FAIL specifications on S_9, counterfactual explanations can be computed exactly as shortest-legal-generator-paths on the orbit graph. The counterfactual distance from state s to spec-flip is the minimum number of legal QA generator moves needed to land in a state where the Boolean specification predicate flips. Claim does NOT generalize to continuous feature-space counterfactual explanations; claim does NOT say the BFS approach scales to arbitrary state spaces. The exactness holds only for QA-discrete finite-state symbolic settings on the QA-discrete side of the Theorem NT firewall. Generator set v1: {qa_step (Fibonacci shift T(b,e) = (e, qa_mod(b+e,9))), scalar_mult_2 ((b,e) → (qa_mod(2b,9), qa_mod(2e,9)) — L_2a coprime-to-9), scalar_mult_3 ((b,e) → (qa_mod(3b,9), qa_mod(3e,9)) — L_2b multiple-of-3, the orbit-class-bridging move per cert [191] tier hierarchy)}. Reuses tools/qa_kg/orbit_failure_enumeration.py orbit_family_s9 (Dale, 2026; cert [263] anchor utility). v1 BFS unweighted; weighted Dijkstra deferred to v2 unless trivial. Sources: (Kochenderfer, 2026) Algorithms for Validation §11.5 'Counterfactual Explanations' (anchored conceptually as an open candidate in bridge §5; not a direct verbatim quote in the Validation excerpts file because §11.5 was tagged not-mapped-here in the v1 ingestion); (Dale, 2026) cert [263] utility + cert [191] tier hierarchy + cert [194] canonical mod-9 classifier. Authored Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-27. Checks CFD_1+DECL/LEGAL/FLIP/MINIMAL/SRC/WIT/F; 2 PASS + 2 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok
